title: The Gang Goes on Family Fight

jokes.text: Charlie asking if this is the green room while standing in the green room
jokes.setup: Charlie expressing wonder about being in a TV green room
jokes.punchline: He doesn't know he's already in the green room he's asking about
jokes.explanation: Perfect Charlie moment - his childlike confusion about basic situations is endearing and character-specific

jokes.text: Frank stealing an ashtray despite not smoking and them having ashtrays at the bar
jokes.setup: Dennis catching Frank stealing something they don't need
jokes.punchline: Frank's petty theft makes no logical sense
jokes.explanation: Classic Frank behavior - petty crime for no reason perfectly fits his character

dialogue.line: Frank stealing an ashtray despite not smoking and them having ashtrays at the bar

jokes.text: Frank wanting to lay out his political agenda on national TV
jokes.setup: The gang trying to behave appropriately on TV
jokes.punchline: Frank immediately wants to be inappropriate and political
jokes.explanation: Frank's complete inability to read the room is perfectly in character

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Frank wanting to lay out his political agenda on national TV

jokes.text: Dee wanting to do a 'Jenny McCarthy thing' - super sexy but with a potty mouth
jokes.setup: Dee trying to create a persona for TV
jokes.punchline: Her completely delusional self-image about being sexy
jokes.explanation: Perfect encapsulation of Dee's delusions about her attractiveness and appeal

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: Dee wanting to do a 'Jenny McCarthy thing' - super sexy but with a potty mouth

jokes.text: Dennis: 'Ah-ah, no, no, Dee, you are never going to be sexy'
jokes.setup: Dee expressing confidence about being sexy
jokes.punchline: Dennis's brutal, matter-of-fact shutdown
jokes.explanation: Dennis's casual cruelty delivered with perfect timing and economy

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: 'Ah-ah, no, no, Dee, you are never going to be sexy'

jokes.text: Dee hasn't eaten in three days to avoid being puffy, comparing herself to J-Mac
jokes.setup: Dee refusing to eat healthy food
jokes.punchline: Her extreme vanity and delusional celebrity comparison
jokes.explanation: Shows Dee's warped priorities and self-image perfectly

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: Dee hasn't eaten in three days to avoid being puffy, comparing herself to J-Mac

jokes.text: Dennis: 'Yeah, because J-Mac's face is stapled to the back of her head'
jokes.setup: Dee comparing herself favorably to a celebrity
jokes.punchline: Dennis's dark explanation for why the celebrity looks good
jokes.explanation: Dark, specific, and unexpected - classic Dennis wit with surgical precision

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: 'Yeah, because J-Mac's face is stapled to the back of her head'

jokes.text: Charlie's fart noise keychain
jokes.setup: Strange noise interrupting the conversation
jokes.punchline: It's Charlie's juvenile toy
jokes.explanation: Juvenile humor but perfectly fits Charlie's childlike character

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Charlie's fart noise keychain

jokes.text: Charlie's complete confusion about cottage cheese - 'cheese from some cottage? Whose cottage?'
jokes.setup: Charlie being offered cottage cheese
jokes.punchline: His literal interpretation of the name
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Charlie logic - taking the name completely literally in a way that's both stupid and weirdly logical

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: cheese from some cottage? Whose cottage?

jokes.text: Charlie claiming he's not a cottage guy despite being a cheese guy
jokes.setup: Someone pointing out Charlie should know about cheese
jokes.punchline: His bizarre distinction between cheese types
jokes.explanation: Charlie's weird expertise boundaries are consistently funny

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: I'm a cheese guy, but I'm not a cottage guy.

jokes.text: Frank's inappropriate 'Sha-booyah roll call' comment to Janet
jokes.setup: Host introducing the families
jokes.punchline: Frank's creepy, inappropriate comment to the opposing team's matriarch
jokes.explanation: Frank being immediately inappropriate on national TV is perfectly in character

jokes.text: Frank wanting to change his answer because he's seen pigs eat men in a bloodbath
jokes.setup: Simple game show question about animals we eat
jokes.punchline: Frank's disturbing personal experience with carnivorous pigs
jokes.explanation: Perfectly dark Frank moment - casual reference to extreme violence delivered matter-of-factly

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Frank wanting to change his answer because he's seen pigs eat men in a bloodbath

jokes.text: Dennis describing himself as proprietor of 'one of the most charming pubs in all of South Philadelphia'
jokes.setup: Host asking Dennis what he does
jokes.punchline: His grandiose description of their terrible bar
jokes.explanation: Dennis's delusional self-promotion is perfectly in character

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: I'm the proprietor of one of the most charming pubs in all of South Philadelphia

jokes.text: Dennis: 'So the question really isn't What do I do? It's What don't I do?'
jokes.setup: Host asking about Dennis's job
jokes.punchline: His incredibly pretentious non-answer
jokes.explanation: Peak Dennis narcissism delivered with complete confidence

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: So the question really isn't What do I do? It's What don't I do?

jokes.text: Dennis answering 'sea urchin' to animals we eat
jokes.setup: Dennis claiming he understands the game perfectly
jokes.punchline: His pretentious answer that normal people wouldn't give
jokes.explanation: Dennis being pretentiously wrong is classic - he thinks he's sophisticated but doesn't understand the game

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: sea urchin

jokes.text: DENNIS: 'His talk of pigs and man flesh is confusing as it is frustrating.'
jokes.setup: Frank's disturbing pig story
jokes.punchline: Dennis's calm, articulate frustration
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dennis - maintaining composure while completely exasperated

dialogue.speaker: DENNIS
dialogue.line: 'His talk of pigs and man flesh is confusing as it is frustrating.'

jokes.text: Dennis's visceral reaction to the buzzer sound - 'Pierces you right down to your soul, doesn't it?'
jokes.setup: Normal game show buzzer sound
jokes.punchline: Dennis's dramatic overreaction to a simple sound
jokes.explanation: Dennis's dramatic sensitivity sets up his eventual breakdown perfectly

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Pierces you right down to your soul, doesn't it?

jokes.text: Dee: 'Well, Grant, I'll tell you what... I like to eat cock.'
jokes.setup: Game show question about animals we eat
jokes.punchline: Dee's inappropriate double entendre
jokes.explanation: Predictable shock humor but fits Dee's desperate attention-seeking

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: Well, Grant, I'll tell you what... I like to eat cock.

jokes.text: Dee explaining her joke: 'It's a little bit of a double entendre type of thing. I was talking about chicken, but I said cock.'
jokes.setup: Dee making an inappropriate joke
jokes.punchline: Her explaining the obvious double entendre
jokes.explanation: Classic Dee - so proud of her 'clever' joke she has to explain it

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: It's a little bit of a double entendre type of thing. I was talking about chicken, but I said cock.

jokes.text: Mac's confusing family explanation - raised them but didn't sire them, might have sired Charlie
jokes.setup: Host asking about their family relationship
jokes.punchline: Mac's unnecessarily complex and disturbing family explanation
jokes.explanation: Unnecessarily complex backstory that confuses everyone including the characters

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: raised them but didn't sire them, might have sired Charlie

jokes.text: DENNIS: 'A sea urchin.' GRANT: 'A sea... a what now?' DENNIS: 'Sea urchin.'
jokes.setup: Question about animals we eat
jokes.punchline: Dennis's pretentious, wrong answer
jokes.explanation: Dennis trying to sound sophisticated but giving a terrible game show answer

dialogue.speaker: DENNIS
dialogue.line: 'A sea urchin.'

dialogue.speaker: GRANT
dialogue.line: 'A sea... a what now?'

dialogue.speaker: DENNIS
dialogue.line: 'Sea urchin.'

jokes.text: Mac bringing a blood bucket for DNA testing during commercial break
jokes.setup: Confusion about family relationships
jokes.punchline: Mac's extreme solution involving blood testing
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully absurd escalation - bringing blood to a game show is peak Sunny

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Mac bringing a blood bucket for DNA testing during commercial break

jokes.text: The host's complete exasperation trying to explain the simple rule to Mac
jokes.setup: Simple game show rule about who says what
jokes.punchline: Mac's complete inability to understand basic instructions
jokes.explanation: Great escalating frustration as a professional host loses it with Mac's stupidity

dialogue.line: The host's complete exasperation trying to explain the simple rule to Mac

jokes.text: CHARLIE: 'Dragon!'
jokes.setup: Question about animals we eat but don't eat us
jokes.punchline: Charlie confidently says dragon
jokes.explanation: Perfect Charlie answer - completely absurd but delivered with total confidence

dialogue.speaker: CHARLIE
dialogue.line: 'Dragon!'

jokes.text: Charlie answering 'Dragon' to animals we eat
jokes.setup: Simple question about animals we eat
jokes.punchline: Charlie's completely fantastical answer
jokes.explanation: Perfect Charlie answer - completely disconnected from reality but delivered with confidence

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Dragon

jokes.text: Charlie's explanation: 'it's not a meal for peasants, it's a meal for kings, and I'm sort of a common man. But they don't eat us; they eat gold and treasure.'
jokes.setup: Host questioning Charlie about eating dragons
jokes.punchline: Charlie's elaborate fantasy logic about dragon dietary habits
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Charlie logic - he's created an entire dragon economy in his head with class distinctions and dietary science

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: it's not a meal for peasants, it's a meal for kings, and I'm sort of a common man. But they don't eat us; they eat gold and treasure.

jokes.text: Dragon actually being on the board with one survey response
jokes.setup: Everyone thinking Charlie's answer is ridiculous
jokes.punchline: Someone actually surveyed said 'dragon'
jokes.explanation: Perfect comedy reversal - the absurd answer is somehow correct, validating Charlie's insanity

dialogue.line: Dragon actually being on the board with one survey response

jokes.text: Dennis complaining that dragon is an answer but sea urchin isn't
jokes.setup: Charlie's ridiculous answer was somehow right
jokes.punchline: Dennis's pretentious answer was wrong while the fantasy was right
jokes.explanation: Nice callback highlighting the absurdity of game show logic vs. Dennis's pretensions

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: dragon is an answer but sea urchin isn't

jokes.text: Charlie claiming he'll get 'the steal' and connecting dragons to dragonflies
jokes.setup: Charlie being successful with his dragon answer
jokes.punchline: His confidence and bizarre logic connecting dragons to dragonflies
jokes.explanation: Charlie's confidence after one success and his weird animal connections are perfectly in character

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: I'm gonna get the steal. I'm gonna get the steal. Look, here's the thing: dragons are real, right? I saw a dragon. I'm pretty sure I saw a dragon. It had a big, long tail, and these big wings. Anyway, dragonflies are just small dragons. So if I find a dragonfly, I'm halfway to my dragon.

jokes.text: Everyone imitating the buzzer sound to annoy Dennis
jokes.setup: Dennis expressing hatred for the buzzer sound
jokes.punchline: Everyone immediately exploiting his weakness
jokes.explanation: Perfect group bullying of Dennis - they immediately weaponize his phobia

dialogue.line: Everyone imitating the buzzer sound to annoy Dennis

jokes.text: Dennis answering 'laser beams' for grooming
jokes.setup: Question about grooming tools
jokes.punchline: Dennis's sci-fi answer about obliterating follicles
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dennis vanity - he'd use futuristic technology for grooming

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Laser beams

jokes.text: Mac suggesting bleaching cream for facial hair
jokes.setup: Trying to think of grooming items
jokes.punchline: Mac's weirdly specific and inappropriate suggestion
jokes.explanation: Oddly specific Mac suggestion that reveals too much about his grooming habits

jokes.text: DENNIS: 'Laser beams, Grant. Yeah, laser beam. It's the only way to completely obliterate the follicle.'
jokes.setup: Question about grooming tools
jokes.punchline: Dennis's over-the-top grooming methods
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dennis vanity - taking grooming to scientific extremes

dialogue.speaker: DENNIS
dialogue.line: 'Laser beams, Grant. Yeah, laser beam. It's the only way to completely obliterate the follicle.'

jokes.text: Charlie thinking 'bride' is a grooming answer because you can't groom without a bride
jokes.setup: Question about grooming items
jokes.punchline: Charlie confusing 'grooming' with 'groom' (wedding)
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Charlie wordplay confusion - he's thinking wedding groom, not grooming yourself

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Charlie thinking 'bride' is a grooming answer because you can't groom without a bride

jokes.text: Charlie suggesting 'toe knife' for grooming with detailed explanation
jokes.setup: Final answer for grooming items
jokes.punchline: Charlie's disgusting personal grooming tool and TMI explanation
jokes.explanation: Peak Charlie grossness - the toe knife is a perfectly disgusting Charlie invention

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: I have a toe knife.

jokes.text: Bride actually being on the board, proving Charlie right
jokes.setup: Everyone dismissing Charlie's confusing answer
jokes.punchline: Charlie was somehow right again
jokes.explanation: Second time Charlie's absurd logic is validated - establishes his weird connection to survey respondents

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Bride actually being on the board, proving Charlie right

jokes.text: Dee breaking the buzzer machine
jokes.setup: Normal game show buzzer use
jokes.punchline: Dee somehow destroys the equipment
jokes.explanation: Simple physical comedy but fits the gang's ability to ruin everything they touch

jokes.text: Host: 'What do you got, hands made of anvils?'
jokes.setup: Dee breaking the buzzer
jokes.punchline: Host's exasperated metaphor
jokes.explanation: Good host reaction to the absurdity - professional breaking down

dialogue.speaker: Host
dialogue.line: What do you got, hands made of anvils?

jokes.text: Frank suggesting 'Liberal yahoos taking my guns' for things people fear
jokes.setup: Question about fears
jokes.punchline: Frank's political rant disguised as a game show answer
jokes.explanation: Frank can't help himself from being political despite Dennis's warnings

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Liberal yahoos taking my guns

jokes.text: Charlie suggesting 'The Nightman' as a fear
jokes.setup: Things people are afraid of
jokes.punchline: Charlie's personal fictional boogeyman
jokes.explanation: Perfect Charlie answer and callback to his musical obsessions

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Charlie suggesting 'The Nightman' as a fear

jokes.text: Dennis suggesting 'the crust on a crème brûlée burning the top of your mouth' but switching to clowns
jokes.setup: Dennis trying to think like a commoner
jokes.punchline: His incredibly specific, pretentious fear vs. obvious answer
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dennis - his natural instinct is absurdly specific and pretentious

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: the crust on a crème brûlée burning the top of your mouth

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: clowns

jokes.text: 'Nightman' actually being on the board as an answer
jokes.setup: Everyone dismissing Charlie's ridiculous answer
jokes.punchline: The Nightman is somehow a legitimate survey response
jokes.explanation: Third validation of Charlie's insane answers - establishes that he somehow understands the weirdos who take surveys

dialogue.line: 'Nightman' actually being on the board as an answer

jokes.text: Nightman actually being on the board and Grant not knowing what it is
jokes.setup: The final answer is revealed
jokes.punchline: Nightman is actually an answer and confuses the host
jokes.explanation: Brilliant payoff - validates Charlie's weird logic again and creates cross-series continuity. Host's confusion adds to the absurdity

dialogue.line: Nightman actually being on the board and Grant not knowing what it is

jokes.text: Charlie revealing he took the survey himself at a mall, thinking it was government
jokes.setup: Mystery of why Charlie keeps getting answers right
jokes.punchline: He was the weirdo who gave those answers in the first place
jokes.explanation: Perfect payoff explaining the mystery - Charlie was the survey respondent all along. Brilliant story structure.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: I took the survey.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: I took it at the mall. I thought it was government.

jokes.text: Cricket demanding his five bucks from Charlie for dancing like a monkey
jokes.setup: Seemingly heartfelt video message
jokes.punchline: Cricket revealing he was paid a pittance to debase himself
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about Cricket's desperation and the gang's exploitation

dialogue.speaker: Cricket
dialogue.line: Where's my five bucks?

jokes.text: Dee fainting from hunger and making fart noises
jokes.setup: Dee starving herself for three days
jokes.punchline: Her body giving out at the worst moment with embarrassing sounds
jokes.explanation: Payoff to Dee's vanity - her extreme dieting backfires spectacularly

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: Dee fainting from hunger and making fart noises

jokes.text: Dennis's complete meltdown during Fast Money, repeatedly saying wrong answers
jokes.setup: Dennis claiming he understands the game perfectly now
jokes.punchline: His complete mental breakdown under pressure
jokes.explanation: Perfect culmination of Dennis's character arc - his need for perfection causes total breakdown

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Dennis's complete meltdown during Fast Money, repeatedly saying wrong answers

jokes.text: Dennis sobbing and repeating 'France' while the buzzer blares
jokes.setup: Dennis already melting down
jokes.punchline: Him getting stuck in a loop of wrong answers
jokes.explanation: The buzzer he hated finally breaks him completely - perfect psychological torture

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: France

jokes.text: Dennis begging them not to air the episode, host saying they always air everything
jokes.setup: Dennis's public humiliation
jokes.punchline: His desperate plea being immediately shut down
jokes.explanation: Classic Dennis - can't handle his image being tarnished, but has no control

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: You can't air this, you can't air this

dialogue.speaker: Host
dialogue.line: We air everything

jokes.text: Dennis's final breakdown insisting 'This doesn't represent me' while sobbing
jokes.setup: Dennis's need to maintain his perfect image
jokes.punchline: His pathetic denial while actively proving the opposite
jokes.explanation: Perfect ending - Dennis's delusion about himself completely shattered by his own actions

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: This doesn't represent me
